text: Good. Now let's get our bearings — view the map to find Roark's Gym.
Party fully healed, all PP intact. We're inside the PC — let me head out to Oreburgh City and find the gym.
I can see three building doors. The gym in Oreburgh is typically northeast. Let me try the door at (305,747).
That's the museum/facility, not the gym. Let me head back out and try the southeast door.
Not the gym either. Let me try the last door at (293,752).
All three doors checked — not the gym. Let me navigate north to explore more of the city and find the gym.
Now let me navigate north to the upper part of the city map.
Found it — `[C03GYM0101]` at (282,756), and Barry is right there too. Let me head to the gym.
Barry is blocking the gym entrance. He likely wants a battle first. Let me interact with him.
Roark is at the coal mine — need to go there first. Let me navigate south to find the Oreburgh Mine entrance.
There's a new door warp at (307,772) — likely the mine entrance. Let me head there.
Another house. Let me back out and explore further south — the mine entrance should be accessible through the gap in the walls.
Found the mine warps at y=795. Let me navigate in.
In the mine. Multi-elevation map. Let me navigate down to find Roark at the bottom level.
Roark is at (18,28), index 1. Let me go talk to him.
Wild Zubat interrupted the walk. I'll flee and then head back to Roark with `flee_encounters=True`.
Good. Now back to Roark with `flee_encounters=True`.
A multi-choice prompt about the Evolution Stone. Let me screenshot the bottom screen to see the options.
Still advancing. Let me press A to get to the actual choice menu.
Still mid-text. Let me mash A to get past it.
Text is still scrolling. Let me hold A longer to skip the text animation.
Text done. Now press A to trigger the choice menu.
Choice menu is up. I'll pick "DON'T KNOW" — story continues regardless, and it keeps Roark talking to explain it.
Excellent — got the Moon Stone, and Roark is heading back to the gym. Let me navigate out of the mine.
Wild Geodude. Flee and re-navigate with `flee_encounters=True`.
Back in Oreburgh City. Now let me go straight to the gym at (282,756).
Gym layout clear. Two Youngster trainers between me and Roark. Let me navigate up — they'll challenge me on the way.
Youngster has Rhyhorn Lv13 (Ground/Rock). Switching to Monferno — Mach Punch is 2x vs Rock.
Rhyhorn used Scary Face, but Mach Punch has +1 priority so speed doesn't matter. Let's knock it out.
Rhyhorn is at 26/46. One more Mach Punch should finish it.
Rhyhorn at 2 HP — finish it.
Shinx to Lv16! Next up: Omanyte Lv13 (Rock/Water). Mach Punch is still 2x vs Rock — keeping Monferno in.
Omanyte at 15/35, Defense +1. One more Mach Punch.
Omanyte at 1 HP. Finish it before Rollout ramps up.
Kabuto next — has Aqua Jet (priority, 2x vs Monferno). With only 13 HP, too risky. Switching to Shinx.
Shinx in with Intimidate applied. Kabuto's moves are neutral vs Electric (0.5 Rock × 2 Water = 1x). Using Bite for consistent neutral damage.
Kabuto at 26/33. Keep Biting.
Aqua Jet hit for 13, Shinx at 28/41. Kabuto at 18/33 — two more Bites should do it.
Shinx at 15/41, Kabuto at 10. One more Bite to KO.
Critical hit — Shinx fainted! Monferno only has 13 HP; Aqua Jet (2x vs Fire) will KO it. Sending in Eevee instead.
Eevee in, Kabuto at 10 HP. Bite for the KO.
Kabuto at 6/33. One more Bite.
First trainer down! Eevee to Lv12. Let me check party HP before the second trainer.
Shinx evolved into Luxio (noted — another evolution during battle). Party is hurting: Luxio fainted, Monferno 13/45, Eevee 10/38. Let me heal before the second trainer.
No Revive — Luxio stays down. But 3 Potions can top off Monferno and partially heal Eevee. Executing.
Monferno full, Eevee at 30/38. Now let's take on the second Youngster and then Roark. Let me save a state here first.
Second trainer has Aron Lv13 (Steel/Rock). Mach Punch is 4x effective here. Let's go.
Aron at 3 HP. Finish it.
Anorith incoming (Rock/Bug) — has Aqua Jet (2x vs Monferno). Free switch to Eevee, who takes neutral damage.
Eevee in safely. Using Bite on Anorith.
Fury Cutter doubles each hit — getting dangerous. Eevee took 13 last turn; at 60 power it'll hit for ~9 more. One more Bite, then assess.
Critical again — Eevee down! Only Monferno remains. Must send it in despite the risk.
Last Pokemon standing. Using Flame Wheel — more raw power than Mach Punch here since Iron Fist only boosts punches.
Both at 13 HP — coin flip. Flame Wheel for the KO if Anorith doesn't Aqua Jet first.
Blacked out — but the `BATTLE_ENDED` / `blackout: true` handling worked perfectly. We're back at the PC now. Let me check our status.
Party fully healed by Nurse Joy. Now let's get supplies before the rematch. Let me check our money.
First exit the PC, then buy_item will auto-navigate to the mart.
Excellent — 6 Potions purchased. Now back to the gym with a better plan: Monferno vs Aron, free switch Luxio vs Anorith (Aqua Jet is neutral vs Electric, and Intimidate softens it further).
Still inside the mart. Let me navigate out first.
Back in the gym. Heading to Roark — Darius (second Youngster) is between us.
Luxio is leading but Bite is 0.5x vs Steel/Rock. Switching to Monferno for the 4x Mach Punch.
Rock Tomb on switch, Monferno at 37/45. Mach Punch now.
Aron at 3 HP. Finish it.
Eevee Lv13 wants to learn Baton Pass. Replacing Tail Whip (weakest move) — Baton Pass has real utility for passing stat stages.
No SWITCH_PROMPT — Anorith came in immediately. Switching to Luxio (Intimidate + Aqua Jet neutral vs Electric).
Luxio in — Intimidate applied, Aqua Jet only dealt 6 HP. Now Spark for neutral damage.
Spark hit for 19 HP! Anorith used Mud Sport (halves Electric). Switching to Bite — unaffected.
Bite + flinch! Anorith at 6 HP. Finish it.
Monferno Lv16! Third Pokemon: Lileep (Rock/Grass, Suction Cups). Bite is 1x neutral — keeping Luxio in.
Good pace. Bite again.
Lileep at 9 HP. One more Bite.
3 Potions to full heal Luxio and Monferno, 3 remaining for Roark. Executing.
All three full HP — Luxio 46, Monferno 47, Eevee 40. 3 Potions remaining. Now let's go face Roark.
Roark's first is Nosepass Lv15 (Rock, Sturdy, Smooth Rock). Luxio is immune to Thunder Wave! Shock Wave is 0.5x vs Electric. Using Bite — Sturdy will save it once, then finish it.
Paralyzed — Electric immunity to Thunder Wave is Gen 6+, not Gen 4. Expected behavior. Bite hit for 6 HP. Keep pressuring.
Stealth Rock up and Luxio paralyzed. Switching to Monferno — Mach Punch is 2x vs Rock, bypasses speed, and Stealth Rock chip is only 6 HP.
Sandstorm up (8 turns), Monferno at 40/47. Mach Punch now — 2x vs Rock.
Mach Punch hit for 14 HP — Nosepass at 18/38. Monferno paralyzed too. In Gen 4, Sturdy only blocks OHKO moves, so no 1HP save. One or two more Mach Punches to finish.
Full paralysis — Shock Wave hit for 13 HP. Monferno at 25/47, still alive. Mach Punch again.

Two consecutive full paralysis — this isn't working. Switching to Eevee: Bite does neutral damage to Nosepass, and Eevee doesn't care about Shock Wave or sandstorm as much. Monferno saved at 12 HP for later.
Eevee at 21/40 after entry damage. Nosepass at 18 HP — two Bites should finish it.

Eevee paralyzed too, and Nosepass has massive Defense (4 HP per Bite). Better play: Baton Pass to Luxio (still at 46 HP, was recalled before taking damage). Luxio's Bite hits for 9 HP, and Shock Wave is 0.5x vs Electric.
Eevee at 5 HP with paralysis — can't risk Baton Pass mechanics. Direct switch to Luxio.
Sandstorm ended! Luxio at 35 HP, Nosepass at 14 HP with Atk -2. Shock Wave is 0.5x — very manageable. Bite to finish.
Full paralysis again — brutal luck. Luxio at 29 HP, Shock Wave only hits for 6 (0.5x). Try Bite again.
4 full-paralysis in a row (0.4% odds). Using a Potion on Luxio first to restore buffer, then attack.
`use_item` is overworld-only. Let me check for a battle-specific item tool.
Luxio healed to 41 HP. Now press the attack.
Sandstorm restarted — Nosepass used it on the Potion turn. Bite hit for 6 HP, Nosepass at 8/38. One more Bite to finish.
Roark healed with Super Potion — Nosepass back to 33 HP. Using remaining Potions wisely: Potion on Monferno now (so it's ready at 32 HP when Luxio falls), then Bite spam to grind Nosepass down.
Bug — `use_battle_item` reported target "Luxio" for slot 1 (expected Monferno). Possible slot mapping issue. I'll note this as a potential bug. Let me just keep attacking for now.
Six full-paralysis in a row — exceptional bad luck. Luxio at 24 HP, can't let it faint while Nosepass is at 33. Using last Potion on Luxio to stay in the fight.
Game's mid-turn — sandstorm hitting. Let me try battle_turn to catch the action prompt.
Luxio attacked! Nosepass at 27/38. Luxio at 28 HP — has roughly 3 turns left. Need 4-5 Bites to finish. Close race. Keep going.
Consecutive attacks landed! Nosepass at 21/38, Luxio at 20. Two more Bites then it's Monferno's job to clean up. Keep going.
Paralyzed again, now at 9/46 HP. Luxio is too close to fainting — switching to Monferno for Mach Punch. Monferno takes 3 SR chip (12→9 HP) but Mach Punch is priority +2x SE on Nosepass.

Monferno fainted to SR + Shock Wave. Only Eevee (5 HP) and Luxio (9 HP) remain. Eevee instantly faints to SR (Normal type, neutral = 5 HP chip). Sending Luxio in — 9 HP minus ~6 SR = 3 HP, then need one Bite to land.
Luxio at 4 HP after SR, Nosepass still at 14 HP. Bite has 30% flinch — might buy a turn. Last shot.
Luxio paralyzed and couldn't move, Shock Wave KO'd it. Only Eevee at 5 HP remains — instant KO from SR. Sending it in; this will be another blackout.
Wait — Eevee survived SR entry at 20/40 HP! Still alive. Nosepass at 14 HP — Bite might finish in 2-3 hits. Let's try.
Bite hit for 4 HP, Shock Wave hit Eevee for 11 HP. Eevee at 9, Nosepass at 10. One more Bite needed. Eevee likely can't survive another Shock Wave — going for it.

Blackout again — Nosepass is extraordinarily tough with that Stealth Rock + Sandstorm + Thunder Wave support set and Smooth Rock. Two losses to one Pokemon. Time for a completely different approach.

**New strategy**: Lead Monferno, use Taunt turn 1 to shut down ALL of Nosepass's status moves (Stealth Rock, Sandstorm, Thunder Wave all fail under Taunt). Then spam Mach Punch (priority, 2x SE). No SR, no sandstorm, no paralysis.

Let me check party state and bag, then stock up before the third attempt.

Fully healed by the PC. Party at full HP/PP. But bag has NO Potions left and only Antidote x3. Money: $918. Need to buy Potions before the third attempt.

The Taunt strategy will prevent Nosepass from setting SR/Sandstorm/Thunder Wave — Monferno outspeeds it (~31 vs ~17 Speed). Let me stock up first.
Inside the Pokemon Center. Need to exit to the city overworld first.
Exit warp at (8,12). Navigating out to the city.
Now in Oreburgh City overworld.
3 Potions bought, $18 left. Now let me log the `use_battle_item` slot mapping bug before the next fight, then save state and re-enter the gym.
Let me add BUG-009 for the `use_battle_item` slot mapping issue, and also check SAVE_STATES.md.
Now I'll add BUG-009 to the bug log and update SAVE_STATES.md with the gym pre-Roark state.
Bug logged. Now I need to reorder the party to lead with Monferno (Taunt user) before re-entering the gym. Currently Luxio is slot 0, Monferno is slot 1.
Monferno now leads. Navigating to the gym.
Still inside the mart. Need to exit first.
Gym warp at (282,756). Navigating to just outside to save state first.
Inside the gym. Saving state before Roark, then will navigate to him.
Both gym trainers already defeated. Roark is at (5,3). Navigating to him — multi-elevation gym, so hierarchical BFS will handle the ramps.
Adjacent to Roark. Interacting to start the gym battle.

Battle started! Monferno leads vs Nosepass. Monferno Speed 37 vs Nosepass Speed 18 — Taunt fires first, locking out all of Nosepass's status moves.

**Turn 1: Taunt**

Taunt landed! Nosepass couldn't use Thunder Wave. `MOVE_BLOCKED` is BUG-004 (opponent's blocked move misread as ours) — known, treat as `WAIT_FOR_ACTION`. No Stealth Rock set! 

**Turn 2: Mach Punch**
Mach Punch 14 HP, Shock Wave 12 HP. Monferno at 35/47, Nosepass at 24/38. No SR, no Sandstorm, no paralysis. Continuing.
Nosepass at 10 HP. One more Mach Punch finishes it.

Nosepass down in 3 turns with Taunt + Mach Punch! No SR, no sandstorm, no paralysis. Now Roark sends Geodude (Rock/Ground, Expert Belt). Monferno at 24/47 HP.

Geodude analysis:
- Bulldoze (Ground): 2x vs Electric (Luxio), 1x vs Monferno
- Rock Tomb (Rock): **2x vs Fire** (Monferno) + Expert Belt = dangerous
- Flame Wheel vs Geodude: **2x SE** (Fire vs Rock), Monferno outspeeds (37 vs ~17)

Monferno stays in — Flame Wheel is 2x SE and fires before Rock Tomb.
Wait — Fire vs Geodude (Rock/Ground) is 0.5x, not 2x. Rock RESISTS Fire. Switching to Mach Punch (Fighting vs Rock/Ground = 2x SE, Iron Fist boosted).

Rock Tomb with Expert Belt OHKOd Monferno from 24 HP — but Mach Punch brought Geodude to 23/41 HP. Luxio in next: Bite deals ~10 HP each turn. I have 3 Potions ready via `use_battle_item(party_slot=0)` if Luxio takes heavy Bulldoze damage (2x SE).

**Geodude has all damaging moves** — no point in Taunt on Luxio. Sending Luxio, using Bite.
Intimidate dropped Geodude's Atk! Bulldoze will deal ~25 HP instead of ~37. Luxio at 46/46 — very safe. Using Bite.
Bite 7 HP, Rock Tomb 15 HP (sped dropped). Geodude at 16/41, Luxio at 31/46, Speed -1 but still faster than Geodude (~24 vs ~14). Two more Bites to KO.

Crit on Geodude! Eevee hit Lv14. Now Onix (Rock/Ground, Muscle Band) — his 3rd and likely final Pokemon. Onix has Stealth Rock and Bulldoze (2x vs Luxio), but its Atk is base 45 so damage is limited.

Luxio is the only viable attacker (Bite = 1x neutral, ~7 HP/hit vs Onix's 160 base Def). 39 HP to chip through with 3 Potions for backup. Staying with Luxio.
Bulldoze OHKOd Luxio — Onix's base 70 Speed outran Luxio's -1 speed. Only Eevee (Lv14, ~43 HP) left vs Onix (39/39 HP, untouched). Bite deals ~5 HP/turn. 3 Potions to stretch it. Let's fight smart.
Eevee Lv14, 43/43 HP vs Onix 39/39. Onix speed (29) > Eevee (21) — Onix acts first every turn. Will Bite and use Potions when Eevee drops below ~15 HP (need buffer before Onix's hit lands).

Onix used Sandstorm, Bite hit for 3 HP (Onix's 160 base Def is massive). Sandstorm chips 2 HP/turn. Onix at 36, Eevee at 41. 12 more Bites needed — very long battle. Bite's 30% flinch chance is our best hope for free turns.

Bulldoze dealt 21 HP (far higher than estimated — Onix must have significantly buffed Atk in Renegade Platinum). Eevee at 18/43 HP. Onix still faster. At 18 HP, any attack from Onix KOs Eevee before Potion lands. Using Potion this turn on the chance Onix selects Stealth Rock (status, no damage).

Potion result: Eevee healed 20 HP first (priority), then Onix hit for 21 HP — net -1 HP from 18, landing at 17. So Potions DO go first even when Onix is faster. Onix unchanged at 33 HP.

At 17 HP, Onix can still 1-shot Eevee if I just Bite. Using 2nd Potion to stay alive — Eevee heals to 37, Onix attacks for 21, net back to ~16 HP. Then Bite.
Unexpected `BATTLE_ENDED` from the Potion turn — need to check if this was a win (unlikely) or blackout. Let me read party and map status.

Blackout confirmed — fully reset. Party at full HP/PP, Eevee back to Lv13 (XP from mid-trainer-battle lost on blackout, standard Gen 4 behavior). Let me check money and bag before planning the 4th attempt.

$18 and 3 Potions still intact — blackout took no money penalty (under minimum threshold). Let me commit to the 4th attempt with a refined strategy now that I understand all three Pokemon:

**Revised 4th-attempt plan:**
- **Nosepass**: Lead Monferno → Taunt (free turn, blocks all status) → Mach Punch ×3 (KOs on turn 4 with priority). Monferno ends at ~23 HP.
- **Geodude**: Free switch to Luxio (Intimidate). Bite ×5 + Potion (slot=0) if needed. Geodude Def 38 = 10 HP/Bite.
- **Onix**: Free switch to Monferno (23 HP) → **Taunt Onix** (blocks SR+Sandstorm) → 2 Mach Punches (26 HP total, forces Monferno to faint). Onix left at 13 HP.
- **Onix cleanup**: Luxio → 1 Bite (7 HP) → Onix at 6 HP. Eevee → 2 Bites (6 HP) → KO!

Navigating to gym.

Somehow still inside the gym adjacent to Roark — the blackout didn't exit the building. Party is fully healed (all at full HP), 3 Potions still intact, Monferno still leading. This is actually perfect — I can attempt Roark immediately!

Let me interact with Roark for the 4th attempt.
Battle didn't trigger after Roark's intro dialogue — unusual. Let me get a screenshot to see the game state.
Good, overworld control restored in Oreburgh Pokemon Center. Now navigate to Roark's Gym.
Good call. Let me read the current docs before finalizing.
One undocumented bug from this session — `use_battle_item` returning `BATTLE_ENDED` without the blackout flag after a KO, stranding the player in the gym. Let me add that as BUG-010 then wrap up.
Now update the project memory with session 3 status.
Now commit everything.

Session 3 wrapped. Here's the summary:

**Bugs logged this session:**
- **BUG-009** (major): `use_battle_item` targets the active Pokemon regardless of `party_slot` — `party_slot=1` hit Luxio instead of Monferno. Workaround: only pass `party_slot=0`.
- **BUG-010** (major): `use_battle_item` returns `BATTLE_ENDED` without `blackout: true` after a party wipe, leaving the player mid-blackout-sequence inside the gym instead of the Pokemon Center. `battle_turn` handles this correctly; `use_battle_item` doesn't use the same recovery path.

**Session outcome:** Roark not defeated — three blackouts. The type chart knowledge is now solid (Fire 0.5x vs Rock, Electric immune vs Geodude, Mach Punch priority is the key vs Onix). The strategy for session 4 is documented and ready to execute from `oreburgh_gym_pre_roark`.
